candidate_archival_state.archival_reason: FAIL_SAFETY at P6 IS (K1 sharpe_proxy/trade<0; K2 expectancy<=0) at S1 baseline; negative in-sample edge despite materially improved risk geometry from the exit/stop redesign

cross_sector_thesis_held_again.interpretation: A7 2.09 / K10 0.4529 in band again (same universe); diversification was never the issue.

exit_mix.note: time-stop fired most (88x): many entries did NOT revert to the mean within the 10-bar horizon, exiting flat-to-negative

profit_loss_ratio.note: P/L ratio 0.877 < 1; avg loss exceeds avg win

exit_stop_redesign_outcome_THE_central_f.hypothesis_tested: s14-d1-cross-sector P7 (LESSON_S14_D1_002/003) attributed the FAIL_SAFETY to the exit/stop geometry (RSI-mid small-winner exit + fixed 2N stop). s15-d1 tested this by replacing BOTH: EXIT-TO-MEAN + 3.5-sigma vol-scaled catastrophe stop (the binding-axis redesign).
exit_stop_redesign_outcome_THE_central_f.interpretation: Fixing the exit/stop materially improved the RISK GEOMETRY (smaller drawdown, smaller loss, rare catastrophe stops) but could NOT manufacture EDGE where the mean-reversion signal lacks one. The dominant failure mode shifted from 2N stop-outs (s14) to time-stops on non-reverting entries (s15). Two consecutive FAIL_SAFETY on the AAPL/JPM/XOM basket using two different mean-reversion mechanics (RSI(3) and z-score) indicate the mean-reversion edge on this universe over 2019-2023 IS appears STRUCTURALLY ABSENT, not merely truncated by a bad stop.

risk_profile_materially_improved.catastrophe_stop_rarely_hit: catastrophe-stop exits = 29 of 180 (16.1%); the 3.5-sigma stop behaved as a disaster brake (NOT a primary exit), exactly as designed

framework_dr10_binding: v2 AND-conjunction (78cd22e); NOT evaluated (P6.5 never reached; FAIL_SAFETY upstream at S1)

lessons_eligible_for_lessons_md_in_a_sep: LESSON_S15_D1_001 (eligible; NOT written this turn): A first-principles exit/stop redesign (exit-to-mean + vol-scaled catastrophe stop) can materially improve a mean-reversion candidate's RISK geometry (drawdown, loss size, stop frequency) without creating EDGE; risk-geometry and edge are separable axes.

LESSON_S15_D1_002 (eligible; NOT written this turn): Two consecutive FAIL_SAFETY on the same universe (AAPL/JPM/XOM) with two different mean-reversion mechanics is strong evidence the mean-reversion edge on that universe/window is structurally absent; a third mean-reversion attempt on the same universe would be framework waste (a candidate T-FORBID).

LESSON_S15_D1_003 (eligible; NOT written this turn): When the s14 hypothesis ('the exit/stop was the failure') is tested by fixing it and the candidate still FAILS, update the diagnosis rather than re-attempting; the next track should change the UNIVERSE or the mechanic FAMILY (e.g., trend/momentum), not iterate mean-reversion exits on the same basket.

WARNING (eligible; NOT written this turn): These lessons would be written to brain_memory/projects/trading_bot/lessons.md only under a separate explicit operator authorization. This P7 memo deliberately does NOT touch lessons.md.

inherited_constraints_block_VERBATIM_FRO: REC1-equivalent (BINDING): OOS K9 reachable on the cross-sector basket (expected ~60-105/y basket-summed). EXIT/STOP-FIRST-PRINCIPLES (BINDING; central thesis): exit = EXIT-TO-MEAN (close on re-cross of SMA_L; the reversion target itself); stop = vol-scaled catastrophe brake (3.5*sigma_L; NOT a fixed 2N). This is the candidate's entire reason to exist after s14-d1-cross-sector FAIL_SAFETY (LESSON_S14_D1_002/003); a hypothesis to TEST at P6 IS, never assumed.

report_kind: S15-D1-CROSS-SECTOR P7 decision memo: formalizes FAIL_SAFETY at P6 IS S1 baseline (K1 sharpe_proxy/trade<0; K2 expectancy<=0). KEY FINDING: the exit/stop redesign (exit-to-mean + 3.5-sigma vol-scaled catastrophe stop) materially improved the risk profile vs s14-d1-cross-sector but did NOT flip the edge positive; two consecutive FAIL_SAFETY on the AAPL/JPM/XOM basket (s14 RSI(3)+2N, s15 z-score+exit-to-mean) indicate the mean-reversion edge on this universe over 2019-2023 IS appears structurally absent. C6 carried verbatim; no_dr_redefinition_post_seal honored.
title: s15-d1-cross-sector z-score exit-to-mean P7 decision memo (sealed; binding; candidate-lifecycle TERMINAL by FAIL_SAFETY at P6 IS)

verdict_drivers.name: sharpe_proxy_per_trade_lt_0
verdict_drivers.precedence_note: K1 is the first-firing gate (safety -> K1 -> K2 -> K4 -> K9). Fires at S1 baseline, UPSTREAM of the DR cost-stress chain; P6.5 never reached.

what_this_outcome_demonstrates: Exit/stop redesign (exit-to-mean + 3.5-sigma vol-scaled catastrophe stop) materially improves the RISK GEOMETRY of a mean-reversion candidate (drawdown halved, loss cut ~63%, catastrophe stop rarely hit) but does NOT, by itself, create per-trade EDGE.

The dominant failure mode shifted from 2N stop-outs (s14) to time-stops on non-reverting entries (s15): the entries simply do not revert to the mean often/far enough on this universe to overcome costs.

Two consecutive FAIL_SAFETY on AAPL/JPM/XOM using two different mean-reversion mechanics (RSI(3)+2N and z-score+exit-to-mean) indicate the mean-reversion edge on THIS universe over 2019-2023 IS appears structurally absent.
FAIL_SAFETY (K1/K2) is fail-closed at S1 baseline and upstream of the DR cost-stress chain; P6.5/P10 are moot. Per SEAL fail_safety_outcomes_terminal, the candidate lifecycle is TERMINAL.
what_this_outcome_does_NOT_demonstrate: It does NOT demonstrate the cross-sector hypothesis was wrong (A7/K10 in band again).
It does NOT demonstrate a K9/insufficient-sample problem (180 trades, ample).

It does NOT fully validate the s14 hypothesis that 'the exit/stop was the problem': the redesign improved risk markedly but the edge remained negative, UPDATING the reading to 'edge appears absent on this universe', not merely 'stop too tight'.
It does NOT condemn z-score mean-reversion universally; THIS sealed spec on THIS universe over THIS IS window failed, and it is locked (no post-SEAL iteration within this candidate).
It does NOT condemn the all-tech sibling DRAFT 214bae0 directly, though that sibling shares a mean-reversion entry on a less-diversified tech basket and is now even less attractive.
